examples: When novelist Rachel Cusk published her memoir of mothering, a Life's Work, in 2001, she had no idea the reaction she would get.
These are the first-known moving images of the abyssal cusk eel – a species of fish that can live deeper in the sea than any other known fish.
As well as the reclusive, skulking fish, the team also found large groups of cusk-eels and crustacean scavengers called amphipods at these depths for the first time.
